title: Making ASCII art in Vim
excerpt: I like using Vim to make ASCII art. I don't use any plugins; Vim already has a bunch of built-in features that are super useful for making ASCII art!
content_html:
I like using Vim to make ASCII art. I don't use any plugins; Vim already has a bunch of built-in features that are super useful for making ASCII art!

     _  _/_Z  _
    / |// / |/ \
    \__/_/_/_/_/

This article is for people who have made ASCII art before and want to see what other tools are out there. If you've never tried making ASCII art before, I recommend you don't read this article! You don't need any of this information to make great ASCII art. Instead, just open up your favorite text editor and start typing. If you're not sure what to make, study the masters and study from life. After that, if you're still curious, then come back and read this.

Vim is not for everyone. I just use it because it's what I know. If you suffer from the same curse, or wish to, then read on!

I recommend having the basics down first (e.g. opening a file, switching between modes, saving and exiting). If you're new to Vim, you can teach yourself the basics with vimtutor!

With all those disclaimers out of the way, let's get into it.

Move your cursor past the end of the line

     .--.        .--.        .--.        .--.
    |@ @ |      |@ @ |      |@ @ |      |o o |
    |    |      |    |      |    |      |~~~ |
    '^^^^'      '^^^^'      '^^^^'      '^^^^'

When drawing ASCII art, you'll often want to move your cursor to open space. Most text editors will not let you do that unless you fill the buffer with spaces first, and indeed Vim behaves that way by default as well. But do:

:set virtualedit=all

Now you can move your cursor beyond the end of the line. When you insert there, Vim automatically fills in the necessary whitespace on the left. Note that while there doesn't need to be a character present for the cursor to move there, there still has to be be a line there. For more information, see:

:help virtualedit

It's also totally valid to not use this feature and just fill the buffer with spaces at the beginning of your session instead; just know that in Vim it's not absolutely necessary. If that's what you want to do, Vim can still help: you can prefix the i and p command with a number to repeat it that many times. For example, you could insert the space character 42 times:

42i<space><esc>

Then copy the current line and paste it 5 times:

yy5p

In any case, it's important for the cursor to move predictably in the direction you tell it to go, or else some of the other techniques in this guide don't work nearly as well.

Multi-line insert

Say, for the sake of argument, you have nyan cat:

     ,----------.
     |  ` .` `. |
     | `. ` `,^----^.
    \| .  `. | @ w @|
     `v-v----"-v-v-"

Suppose you want to draw some squiggles behind nyan cat, but there's not enough space on the left, so you also want to shift nyan cat over to the right. How would you go about that?

Here's one way: Enter Visual block mode with Ctrl+v and select the column behind nyan cat. Then press Shift+i and type some squiggles. You'll notice while you're typing that it looks like you're only inserting on the first line, but that is expected. Only after you press the Escape key will you see that you've actually inserted on all the lines of your selection!

    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ,----------.
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ |  ` .` `. |
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ | `. ` `,^----^.
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\| .  `. | @ w @|
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ `v-v----"-v-v-"

In this case I actually did:

<ctrl+v>jjjj32I~<esc>

I'll admit this is a pretty contrived example. I usually just use this technique to insert whitespace on multiple lines at once to widen a box, or move something to the right.

Fill/delete rectangular region

Suppose you have this dirty box, and you want to clean it up.

    +--------+
    |  `  ~  |
    | * ` .` |
    |  . ^ . |
    +--------+

Here's one way: enter Visual block mode with Ctrl+v. Select the region inside the box, press r, then press space.

    +--------+
    |        |
    |        |
    |        |
    +--------+

All done! I often use this technique to erase a section (e.g. if I want to redo it) without accidentally disturbing another part of the drawing. If I'd pressed d instead of r, I would have gotten this:

    +--------+
    ||
    ||
    ||
    +--------+

Sometimes that's desirable, but in this case not what I wanted.

Copy and paste rectangular regions

                   +----+
    +----+         | +----+
    |    |         +-| +----+
    +----+           +-|    |
                       +----+

In Visual block mode, you can also copy and paste. To copy the current selection to the clipboard, press y (this puts you back in Normal mode). To paste, press p or P.

Pasting a block in Normal mode "inserts" it; anything to the right is pushed further to the right.

                      .----.
                     | @  @ |
                     | .--. |
    +---------+               .--` <> '--.
    |         |               `--. <> .--'
    +---------+                 /  <>  \
                    /  .--.  \
                    `-'    `-'

I usually want to paste "over" what's already there, without shifting anything. To do that, after copying a block, move the cursor somewhere else and try:

1vP

That should have effectively overwritten whatever was there before. To break that down, 1v is a special variant of v that creates a selection with the dimensions of your last Visual mode operation. Then, P does two things: it deletes whatever's selected, then pastes. I recommend P over p because it doesn't clobber the clipboard, so you can repeat the paste if you want. For more information on this difference, see:

:help v_P

                      .----.
                     | @  @ |
                     | \__/ |
    +---------+    .--` <> '--.
    |         |    `--. <> .--'
    +---------+      /  <>  \
                    /  .--.  \
                    `-'    `-'

Macros

Vim allows you to record a sequence of keystrokes and play it back repeatedly. This can be more powerful than pressing the . key, which can only repeat single commands.

One simple way to get started with macros is to combine insertion with movement. Let me show you what I mean. In this example, I have this 7x3 tile that I'd like to repeat in a diagonal fashion.

     ,.-~.         +-----+
    `   _+"   -->  | 7x3 |
       o;.-        +-----+

It should tile like so:

    +-----+
    |  1  |+-----+
    +-----+|  2  |+-----+
           +-----+|  3  |  ...
                  +-----+

Forgetting about macros for a moment, I would do this by copying the tile using Visual block mode, move the cursor, paste, move the cursor again, paste again. The result:

      ,.-~. 
     `   _+" ,.-~. 
        o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
               o;.-`   _+"
                      o;.-

In this scenario, it's perfectly reasonable to do without macros, but what if I wanted to paste it 10 or 100 times? Maybe a macro is actually worth trying in that instance.

First I'll copy the tile to the clipboard. Then, I'll position the cursor where I want to start pasting. Then I'll start recording a macro into the 'w' register ('w' is an arbitrary choice, pick whatever register you like):

qw

I'll paste, then move to the next paste location:

1vP<esc>7lj

Finally, I'll stop the recording:

q

Cool, now I have a repeatable macro. To play it back, I just have to do:

@w

And now that it's a macro, I can do it n times:

8@w

      ,.-~. 
     `   _+" ,.-~. 
        o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
               o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                      o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                             o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                                    o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                                           o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                                                  o;.-`   _+"
                                                         o;.-

To be honest, I don't use macros a ton when making ASCII art. It's rare that I need to repeat a pattern that many times. Plus, it's easy to program the macro wrong and end up wasting more time than if I'd just done the job manually.

Here's another example:

                          /\
                     /\/\/  \
      /\            /        \
     /  \/\        /          \  /\                  /\
    /      \/\    /            \/  \/\/\        /\/\/  \
              \/\/                      \/\    /        \
                                           \  /
                                            \/

For this, I used two macros to make my life a bit easier.

The first macro draws a / and moves the cursor up and to the right:

r/kl

The second macro moves the cursor down, draws a \, and moves the cursor to the right:

jr\l

These macros ensure that my cursor is always positioned at the rightmost end of the line, ready to draw the next segment.

For more info on macros, see:

:help q

Mouse support

    .
    :`.
    :  `.
    :   .:.
    :.'\\
    `   \\
         `

By mouse support, I just mean that you can click anywhere in your Vim window to move the cursor there. Useful for drawing pictures! Anyway, you probably knew this; it's usually on by default. If it's off, you can turn it on with:

:set mouse=a

It also works in the terminal! This might surprise you if you haven't used Vim since *checks notes* 1994, or if you're coming from the original vi editor.

Make whitespace visible

In the process of creating your ASCII art, you might lose track of your whitespace. Not a big deal, but if for some reason you want to know what your whitespace looks like, you can ask Vim to make your whitespace visible by doing:

:set list

You should see dollar signs $ demarcating the ends of lines. You can have Vim visualize other kinds of whitespace as well. See:

:help listchars

Parting words

When asked, "do you need any special programs or software to make ASCII Art?", the great ASCII artist Joan G. Stark said:

All you need is a text editor with a fixed-width font.

I tend to agree. You don't need to know any tricks to make good ASCII art. If you've decided to make art, a lack of tools isn't going to stop you. In writing this, I don't mean to suggest that these tools are some kind of secret sauce. That's got to come from within you.

         (___)
         (ovo)
         /vvv\
     ayc |vvv|
    ======w=w===

blocks.html_en: Here's one way: Enter Visual block mode with Ctrl+v and select the column behind nyan cat. Then press Shift+i and type some squiggles. sentences.en: Here's one way: Enter Visual block mode with Ctrl+v and select the column behind nyan cat.

sentences.en: Then press Shift+i and type some squiggles.
sentences.zh: 然后按 Shift+i 并输入一些曲线。

sentences.en: You'll notice while you're typing that it looks like you're only inserting on the first line, but that is expected.
sentences.zh: 您在键入时会注意到，看起来您只在第一行插入，但这是预期的。

sentences.en: Only after you press the Escape key will you see that you've actually inserted on all the lines of your selection!
sentences.zh: 只有在按下 Escape 键后，您才会看到您实际上已经插入了所选的所有行！

blocks.html_en: In this case I actually did:

sentences.en: In this case I actually did:
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下我实际上做了：

blocks.html_en: I'll admit this is a pretty contrived example. I usually just use this technique to insert whitespace on multiple lines at once to widen a box, or move something to the right.

sentences.en: I'll admit this is a pretty contrived example.
sentences.zh: 我承认这是一个非常人为的例子。

sentences.en: I usually just use this technique to insert whitespace on multiple lines at once to widen a box, or move something to the right.
sentences.zh: 我通常只是使用这种技术一次在多行上插入空格以加宽框，或将某些内容向右移动。

blocks.html_en: Fill/delete rectangular region

sentences.en: Fill/delete rectangular region
sentences.zh: 填充/删除矩形区域

blocks.html_en: Suppose you have this dirty box, and you want to clean it up.

sentences.en: Suppose you have this dirty box, and you want to clean it up.
sentences.zh: 假设你有一个脏盒子，你想清理它。

blocks.html_en: Here's one way: enter Visual block mode with Ctrl+v. Select the region inside the box, press r, then press space.

sentences.en: Here's one way: enter Visual block mode with Ctrl+v.
sentences.zh: 一种方法是：使用 Ctrl+v 进入可视块模式。

sentences.en: Select the region inside the box, press r, then press space.
sentences.zh: 选择框内的区域，按 r，然后按空格键。

blocks.html_en: All done! I often use this technique to erase a section (e.g. if I want to redo it) without accidentally disturbing another part of the drawing. If I'd pressed d instead of r, I would have gotten this:

sentences.en: All done!
sentences.zh: 全部完成！

sentences.en: I often use this technique to erase a section (e.g. if I want to redo it) without accidentally disturbing another part of the drawing.
sentences.zh: 我经常使用这种技术来擦除一个部分（例如，如果我想重做它），而不会意外地干扰绘图的其他部分。

sentences.en: If I'd pressed d instead of r, I would have gotten this:
sentences.zh: 如果我按 d 而不是 r，我会得到这个：

blocks.html_en: Sometimes that's desirable, but in this case not what I wanted.

sentences.en: Sometimes that's desirable, but in this case not what I wanted.
sentences.zh: 有时这是可取的，但在这种情况下不是我想要的。

blocks.html_en: Copy and paste rectangular regions

sentences.en: Copy and paste rectangular regions
sentences.zh: 复制并粘贴矩形区域

blocks.html_en: In Visual block mode, you can also copy and paste. To copy the current selection to the clipboard, press y (this puts you back in Normal mode). To paste, press p or P.

sentences.en: In Visual block mode, you can also copy and paste.
sentences.zh: 在可视块模式下，您还可以复制和粘贴。

sentences.en: To copy the current selection to the clipboard, press y (this puts you back in Normal mode).
sentences.zh: 要将当前选择复制到剪贴板，请按 y （这将使您返回正常模式）。

sentences.en: To paste, press p or P.
sentences.zh: 要粘贴，请按 p 或 P。

blocks.html_en: Pasting a block in Normal mode "inserts" it; anything to the right is pushed further to the right.

sentences.en: Pasting a block in Normal mode "inserts" it; anything to the right is pushed further to the right.
sentences.zh: 在普通模式下粘贴块会“插入”它；右边的任何东西都会被进一步推到右边。

blocks.html_en: I usually want to paste "over" what's already there, without shifting anything. To do that, after copying a block, move the cursor somewhere else and try:

sentences.en: I usually want to paste "over" what's already there, without shifting anything.
sentences.zh: 我通常想“粘贴”已有的内容，而不移动任何内容。

sentences.en: To do that, after copying a block, move the cursor somewhere else and try:
sentences.zh: 为此，复制块后，将光标移动到其他位置并尝试：

blocks.html_en: That should have effectively overwritten whatever was there before. To break that down, 1v is a special variant of v that creates a selection with the dimensions of your last Visual mode operation. Then, P does two things: it deletes whatever's selected, then pastes. I recommend P over p because it doesn't clobber the clipboard, so you can repeat the paste if you want. For more information on this difference, see:

sentences.en: That should have effectively overwritten whatever was there before.
sentences.zh: 这应该有效地覆盖了之前存在的任何内容。

sentences.en: To break that down, 1v is a special variant of v that creates a selection with the dimensions of your last Visual mode operation.
sentences.zh: 详细来说，1v 是 v 的一个特殊变体，它使用上次可视化模式操作的尺寸创建一个选择。

sentences.en: Then, P does two things: it deletes whatever's selected, then pastes.
sentences.zh: 然后，P 执行两件事：删除所选内容，然后粘贴。

sentences.en: I recommend P over p because it doesn't clobber the clipboard, so you can repeat the paste if you want.
sentences.zh: 我推荐 P 而不是 p，因为它不会破坏剪贴板，因此您可以根据需要重复粘贴。

sentences.en: For more information on this difference, see:
sentences.zh: 有关此差异的更多信息，请参阅：

blocks.html_en: Macros

sentences.en: Macros
sentences.zh: 宏

blocks.html_en: Vim allows you to record a sequence of keystrokes and play it back repeatedly. This can be more powerful than pressing the . key, which can only repeat single commands.

sentences.en: Vim allows you to record a sequence of keystrokes and play it back repeatedly.
sentences.zh: Vim 允许您记录一系列击键并重复播放。

sentences.en: This can be more powerful than pressing the .
sentences.zh: 这比按 更有效。

sentences.en: key, which can only repeat single commands.
sentences.zh: 键，只能重复单个命令。

blocks.html_en: One simple way to get started with macros is to combine insertion with movement. Let me show you what I mean. In this example, I have this 7x3 tile that I'd like to repeat in a diagonal fashion.

sentences.en: One simple way to get started with macros is to combine insertion with movement.
sentences.zh: 开始使用宏的一种简单方法是将插入与移动结合起来。

sentences.en: Let me show you what I mean.
sentences.zh: 让我告诉你我的意思。

sentences.en: In this example, I have this 7x3 tile that I'd like to repeat in a diagonal fashion.
sentences.zh: 在此示例中，我有一个 7x3 的图块，我想以对角线方式重复它。

blocks.html_en: It should tile like so:

sentences.en: It should tile like so:
sentences.zh: 它应该像这样平铺：

blocks.html_en: Forgetting about macros for a moment, I would do this by copying the tile using Visual block mode, move the cursor, paste, move the cursor again, paste again. The result:

sentences.en: Forgetting about macros for a moment, I would do this by copying the tile using Visual block mode, move the cursor, paste, move the cursor again, paste again.
sentences.zh: 暂时忘记宏，我会通过使用可视块模式复制图块，移动光标，粘贴，再次移动光标，再次粘贴来完成此操作。

sentences.en: The result:
sentences.zh: 结果：

blocks.html_en: In this scenario, it's perfectly reasonable to do without macros, but what if I wanted to paste it 10 or 100 times? Maybe a macro is actually worth trying in that instance.

sentences.en: In this scenario, it's perfectly reasonable to do without macros, but what if I wanted to paste it 10 or 100 times?
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，不使用宏是完全合理的，但是如果我想粘贴它 10 或 100 次怎么办？

sentences.en: Maybe a macro is actually worth trying in that instance.
sentences.zh: 也许在这种情况下宏实际上值得尝试。

blocks.html_en: First I'll copy the tile to the clipboard. Then, I'll position the cursor where I want to start pasting. Then I'll start recording a macro into the 'w' register ('w' is an arbitrary choice, pick whatever register you like):

sentences.en: First I'll copy the tile to the clipboard.
sentences.zh: 首先，我会将图块复制到剪贴板。

sentences.en: Then, I'll position the cursor where I want to start pasting.
sentences.zh: 然后，我将光标定位在要开始粘贴的位置。

sentences.en: Then I'll start recording a macro into the 'w' register ('w' is an arbitrary choice, pick whatever register you like):
sentences.zh: 然后我将开始将宏记录到“w”寄存器中（“w”是任意选择，选择您喜欢的任何寄存器）：

blocks.html_en: I'll paste, then move to the next paste location:

sentences.en: I'll paste, then move to the next paste location:
sentences.zh: 我将粘贴，然后移动到下一个粘贴位置：

blocks.html_en: Finally, I'll stop the recording:

sentences.en: Finally, I'll stop the recording:
sentences.zh: 最后，我将停止录制：

blocks.html_en: Cool, now I have a repeatable macro. To play it back, I just have to do:

sentences.en: Cool, now I have a repeatable macro.
sentences.zh: 酷，现在我有了一个可重复的宏。

sentences.en: To play it back, I just have to do:
sentences.zh: 要回放它，我只需要做：

blocks.html_en: And now that it's a macro, I can do it n times:

sentences.en: And now that it's a macro, I can do it n times:
sentences.zh: 现在它是一个宏，我可以执行 n 次：

blocks.html_en: To be honest, I don't use macros a ton when making ASCII art. It's rare that I need to repeat a pattern that many times. Plus, it's easy to program the macro wrong and end up wasting more time than if I'd just done the job manually.

sentences.en: To be honest, I don't use macros a ton when making ASCII art.
sentences.zh: 老实说，我在制作 ASCII 艺术时并没有大量使用宏。

sentences.en: It's rare that I need to repeat a pattern that many times.
sentences.zh: 我很少需要多次重复同一个模式。

sentences.en: Plus, it's easy to program the macro wrong and end up wasting more time than if I'd just done the job manually.
sentences.zh: 另外，很容易对宏进行错误编程，最终比我手动完成这项工作浪费更多的时间。

blocks.html_en: Here's another example:

sentences.en: Here's another example:
sentences.zh: 这是另一个例子：

blocks.html_en: For this, I used two macros to make my life a bit easier.

sentences.en: For this, I used two macros to make my life a bit easier.
sentences.zh: 为此，我使用了两个宏来让我的生活更轻松一些。

blocks.html_en: The first macro draws a / and moves the cursor up and to the right:

sentences.en: The first macro draws a / and moves the cursor up and to the right:
sentences.zh: 第一个宏绘制一个 / 并将光标向上向右移动：

blocks.html_en: The second macro moves the cursor down, draws a \, and moves the cursor to the right:

sentences.en: The second macro moves the cursor down, draws a \, and moves the cursor to the right:
sentences.zh: 第二个宏将光标向下移动，绘制一个 \，然后将光标向右移动：

blocks.html_en: These macros ensure that my cursor is always positioned at the rightmost end of the line, ready to draw the next segment.

sentences.en: These macros ensure that my cursor is always positioned at the rightmost end of the line, ready to draw the next segment.
sentences.zh: 这些宏确保我的光标始终位于该行的最右端，准备绘制下一段。

blocks.html_en: For more info on macros, see:

sentences.en: For more info on macros, see:
sentences.zh: 有关宏的更多信息，请参阅：

blocks.html_en: Mouse support

sentences.en: Mouse support
sentences.zh: 鼠标支持

blocks.html_en: By mouse support, I just mean that you can click anywhere in your Vim window to move the cursor there. Useful for drawing pictures! Anyway, you probably knew this; it's usually on by default. If it's off, you can turn it on with:

sentences.en: By mouse support, I just mean that you can click anywhere in your Vim window to move the cursor there.
sentences.zh: 我所说的鼠标支持只是指您可以单击 Vim 窗口中的任意位置来将光标移动到那里。

sentences.en: Useful for drawing pictures!
sentences.zh: 画画很有用！

sentences.en: Anyway, you probably knew this; it's usually on by default.
sentences.zh: 不管怎样，你可能知道这一点；它通常是默认打开的。

sentences.en: If it's off, you can turn it on with:
sentences.zh: 如果它已关闭，您可以通过以下方式将其打开：

blocks.html_en: It also works in the terminal! This might surprise you if you haven't used Vim since *checks notes* 1994, or if you're coming from the original vi editor.

sentences.en: It also works in the terminal!
sentences.zh: 它也可以在终端中运行！

sentences.en: This might surprise you if you haven't used Vim since *checks notes* 1994, or if you're coming from the original vi editor.
sentences.zh: 如果您自 *checksnotes* 1994 年以来就没有使用过 Vim，或者如果您来自最初的 vi 编辑器，这可能会让您感到惊讶。

blocks.html_en: Make whitespace visible

sentences.en: Make whitespace visible
sentences.zh: 使空白可见

blocks.html_en: In the process of creating your ASCII art, you might lose track of your whitespace. Not a big deal, but if for some reason you want to know what your whitespace looks like, you can ask Vim to make your whitespace visible by doing:

sentences.en: In the process of creating your ASCII art, you might lose track of your whitespace.
sentences.zh: 在创建 ASCII 艺术作品的过程中，您可能会忘记空白。

sentences.en: Not a big deal, but if for some reason you want to know what your whitespace looks like, you can ask Vim to make your whitespace visible by doing:
sentences.zh: 没什么大不了的，但如果出于某种原因你想知道你的空白是什么样子，你可以要求 Vim 通过执行以下操作来使你的空白可见：

blocks.html_en: You should see dollar signs $ demarcating the ends of lines. You can have Vim visualize other kinds of whitespace as well. See:

sentences.en: You should see dollar signs $ demarcating the ends of lines.
sentences.zh: 您应该看到美元符号 $ 来划分行尾。

sentences.en: You can have Vim visualize other kinds of whitespace as well.
sentences.zh: 您还可以让 Vim 可视化其他类型的空白。

sentences.en: See:
sentences.zh: 看：

blocks.html_en: Parting words

sentences.en: Parting words
sentences.zh: 临别赠言

blocks.html_en: When asked, "do you need any special programs or software to make ASCII Art?", the great ASCII artist Joan G. Stark said:

sentences.en: When asked, "do you need any special programs or software to make ASCII Art?", the great ASCII artist Joan G.
sentences.zh: 当被问到“你需要任何特殊程序或软件来制作 ASCII 艺术吗？”时，伟大的 ASCII 艺术家 Joan G.

sentences.en: Stark said:
sentences.zh: 斯塔克说：

blocks.html_en: All you need is a text editor with a fixed-width font.

sentences.en: All you need is a text editor with a fixed-width font.
sentences.zh: 您所需要的只是一个具有固定宽度字体的文本编辑器。

blocks.html_en: All you need is a text editor with a fixed-width font.

sentences.en: All you need is a text editor with a fixed-width font.
sentences.zh: 您所需要的只是一个具有固定宽度字体的文本编辑器。

blocks.html_en: I tend to agree. You don't need to know any tricks to make good ASCII art. If you've decided to make art, a lack of tools isn't going to stop you. In writing this, I don't mean to suggest that these tools are some kind of secret sauce. That's got to come from within you.

sentences.en: I tend to agree.
sentences.zh: 我倾向于同意。

sentences.en: You don't need to know any tricks to make good ASCII art.
sentences.zh: 您无需了解任何技巧即可制作出色的 ASCII 艺术作品。

sentences.en: If you've decided to make art, a lack of tools isn't going to stop you.
sentences.zh: 如果您决定创作艺术，那么缺乏工具不会阻止您。

sentences.en: In writing this, I don't mean to suggest that these tools are some kind of secret sauce.
sentences.zh: 在写这篇文章时，我并不是想暗示这些工具是某种秘密武器。

sentences.en: That's got to come from within you.
sentences.zh: 那必须来自你的内心。

content_html_zh:
我喜欢使用 Vim 制作 ASCII 艺术。 我不使用任何插件； Vim 已经有很多内置功能，对于制作 ASCII 艺术非常有用！

     _  _/_Z  _
    / |// / |/ \
    \__/_/_/_/_/

本文适合以前制作过 ASCII 艺术并想了解还有哪些其他工具的人。 如果您以前从未尝试过制作 ASCII 艺术，我建议您不要阅读这篇文章！ 您不需要任何这些信息来制作出色的 ASCII 艺术作品。 相反，只需打开您最喜欢的文本编辑器并开始输入即可。 如果你不确定要做什么，那就向大师学习，从生活中学习。 之后，如果您仍然好奇，请回来阅读本文。

Vim 并不适合所有人。 我只是使用它，因为这是我所知道的。 如果您遭受同样的诅咒，或者希望这样做，那么请继续阅读！

我建议首先了解基础知识（例如打开文件、在模式之间切换、保存和退出）。 如果您是 Vim 新手，您可以使用 vimtutor 自学基础知识！

抛开所有这些免责声明，让我们开始吧。

将光标移过行尾

     .--.        .--.        .--.        .--.
    |@ @ |      |@ @ |      |@ @ |      |o o |
    |    |      |    |      |    |      |~~~ |
    '^^^^'      '^^^^'      '^^^^'      '^^^^'

绘制 ASCII 艺术时，您经常需要将光标移动到空白处。 大多数文本编辑器不会让你这样做，除非你先用空格填充缓冲区，事实上 Vim 默认情况下也是这样做的。 但要这样做：

:set virtualedit=all

现在您可以将光标移到行尾之外。 当您在此处插入时，Vim 会自动填充左侧必要的空白。 请注意，虽然不需要存在字符来让光标移动到那里，但那里仍然必须有一行。 有关更多信息，请参阅：

:help virtualedit

不使用此功能并在会话开始时用空格填充缓冲区也是完全有效的；只是知道在 Vim 中这并不是绝对必要的。 如果这就是您想要做的，Vim 仍然可以提供帮助：您可以在 i 和 p 命令前面加上一个数字来重复执行多次。 例如，您可以插入空格字符 42 次：

42i<space><esc>

然后复制当前行并粘贴5次：

yy5p

无论如何，重要的是光标要按照您指定的方向可预测地移动，否则本指南中的一些其他技术几乎不起作用。

多行插入

为了便于讨论，假设你有 nyan 猫：

     ,----------.
     |  ` .` `. |
     | `. ` `,^----^.
    \| .  `. | @ w @|
     `v-v----"-v-v-"

假设您想在 nyan cat 后面画一些曲线，但左侧没有足够的空间，因此您还想将 nyan cat 移到右侧。 你会怎么做呢？

一种方法是：使用 Ctrl+v 进入可视块模式，然后选择 nyan cat 后面的列。 然后按 Shift+i 并输入一些曲线。 您在键入时会注意到，看起来您只在第一行插入，但这是预期的。 只有在按下 Escape 键后，您才会看到您实际上已经插入了所选的所有行！

    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ,----------.
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ |  ` .` `. |
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ | `. ` `,^----^.
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\| .  `. | @ w @|
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ `v-v----"-v-v-"

在这种情况下我实际上做了：

<ctrl+v>jjjj32I~<esc>

我承认这是一个非常人为的例子。 我通常只是使用这种技术一次在多行上插入空格以加宽框，或将某些内容向右移动。

填充/删除矩形区域

假设你有一个脏盒子，你想清理它。

    +--------+
    |  `  ~  |
    | * ` .` |
    |  . ^ . |
    +--------+

一种方法是：使用 Ctrl+v 进入可视块模式。 选择框内的区域，按 r，然后按空格键。

    +--------+
    |        |
    |        |
    |        |
    +--------+

全部完成！ 我经常使用这种技术来擦除一个部分（例如，如果我想重做它），而不会意外地干扰绘图的其他部分。 如果我按 d 而不是 r，我会得到这个：

    +--------+
    ||
    ||
    ||
    +--------+

有时这是可取的，但在这种情况下不是我想要的。

复制并粘贴矩形区域

                   +----+
    +----+         | +----+
    |    |         +-| +----+
    +----+           +-|    |
                       +----+

在可视块模式下，您还可以复制和粘贴。 要将当前选择复制到剪贴板，请按 y （这将使您返回正常模式）。 要粘贴，请按 p 或 P。

在普通模式下粘贴块会“插入”它；右边的任何东西都会被进一步推到右边。

                      .----.
                     | @  @ |
                     | .--. |
    +---------+               .--` <> '--.
    |         |               `--. <> .--'
    +---------+                 /  <>  \
                    /  .--.  \
                    `-'    `-'

我通常想“粘贴”已有的内容，而不移动任何内容。 为此，复制块后，将光标移动到其他位置并尝试：

1vP

这应该有效地覆盖了之前存在的任何内容。 详细来说，1v 是 v 的一个特殊变体，它使用上次可视化模式操作的尺寸创建一个选择。 然后，P 执行两件事：删除所选内容，然后粘贴。 我推荐 P 而不是 p，因为它不会破坏剪贴板，因此您可以根据需要重复粘贴。 有关此差异的更多信息，请参阅：

:help v_P

                      .----.
                     | @  @ |
                     | \__/ |
    +---------+    .--` <> '--.
    |         |    `--. <> .--'
    +---------+      /  <>  \
                    /  .--.  \
                    `-'    `-'

宏

Vim 允许您记录一系列击键并重复播放。 这比按 更有效。 键，只能重复单个命令。

开始使用宏的一种简单方法是将插入与移动结合起来。 让我告诉你我的意思。 在此示例中，我有一个 7x3 的图块，我想以对角线方式重复它。

     ,.-~.         +-----+
    `   _+"   -->  | 7x3 |
       o;.-        +-----+

它应该像这样平铺：

    +-----+
    |  1  |+-----+
    +-----+|  2  |+-----+
           +-----+|  3  |  ...
                  +-----+

暂时忘记宏，我会通过使用可视块模式复制图块，移动光标，粘贴，再次移动光标，再次粘贴来完成此操作。 结果：

      ,.-~. 
     `   _+" ,.-~. 
        o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
               o;.-`   _+"
                      o;.-

在这种情况下，不使用宏是完全合理的，但是如果我想粘贴它 10 或 100 次怎么办？ 也许在这种情况下宏实际上值得尝试。

首先，我会将图块复制到剪贴板。 然后，我将光标定位在要开始粘贴的位置。 然后我将开始将宏记录到“w”寄存器中（“w”是任意选择，选择您喜欢的任何寄存器）：

qw

我将粘贴，然后移动到下一个粘贴位置：

1vP<esc>7lj

最后，我将停止录制：

q

酷，现在我有了一个可重复的宏。 要回放它，我只需要做：

@w

现在它是一个宏，我可以执行 n 次：

8@w

      ,.-~. 
     `   _+" ,.-~. 
        o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
               o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                      o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                             o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                                    o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                                           o;.-`   _+" ,.-~. 
                                                  o;.-`   _+"
                                                         o;.-

老实说，我在制作 ASCII 艺术时并没有大量使用宏。 我很少需要多次重复同一个模式。 另外，很容易对宏进行错误编程，最终比我手动完成这项工作浪费更多的时间。

这是另一个例子：

                          /\
                     /\/\/  \
      /\            /        \
     /  \/\        /          \  /\                  /\
    /      \/\    /            \/  \/\/\        /\/\/  \
              \/\/                      \/\    /        \
                                           \  /
                                            \/

为此，我使用了两个宏来让我的生活更轻松一些。

第一个宏绘制一个 / 并将光标向上向右移动：

r/kl

第二个宏将光标向下移动，绘制一个 \，然后将光标向右移动：

jr\l

这些宏确保我的光标始终位于该行的最右端，准备绘制下一段。

有关宏的更多信息，请参阅：

:help q

鼠标支持

    .
    :`.
    :  `.
    :   .:.
    :.'\\
    `   \\
         `

我所说的鼠标支持只是指您可以单击 Vim 窗口中的任意位置来将光标移动到那里。 画画很有用！ 不管怎样，你可能知道这一点；它通常是默认打开的。 如果它已关闭，您可以通过以下方式将其打开：

:set mouse=a

它也可以在终端中运行！ 如果您自 *checksnotes* 1994 年以来就没有使用过 Vim，或者如果您来自最初的 vi 编辑器，这可能会让您感到惊讶。

使空白可见

在创建 ASCII 艺术作品的过程中，您可能会忘记空白。 没什么大不了的，但如果出于某种原因你想知道你的空白是什么样子，你可以要求 Vim 通过执行以下操作来使你的空白可见：

:set list

您应该看到美元符号 $ 来划分行尾。 您还可以让 Vim 可视化其他类型的空白。 看：

:help listchars

临别赠言

当被问到“你需要任何特殊程序或软件来制作 ASCII 艺术吗？”时，伟大的 ASCII 艺术家 Joan G. 斯塔克说：

您所需要的只是一个具有固定宽度字体的文本编辑器。

我倾向于同意。 您无需了解任何技巧即可制作出色的 ASCII 艺术作品。 如果您决定创作艺术，那么缺乏工具不会阻止您。 在写这篇文章时，我并不是想暗示这些工具是某种秘密武器。 那必须来自你的内心。

         (___)
         (ovo)
         /vvv\
     ayc |vvv|
    ======w=w===
